title: malus fusca var. diversifolia
intro: Malus fusca var. diversifolia, also known as the wild apple or western wild apple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Malus fusca var. diversifolia include its ability to support respiratory health, reduce fever, and aid in digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
